name: docs_export
purpose: Generate a standalone Node.js script (scripts/exportDocs.js) that exports all viewer documents to MkDocs or Confluence. The script calls the running viewer's REST API to fetch the document tree, templates, and content — then renders each doc through its Mustache template and writes output files.

viewerApi.note: Live viewer REST API — must be running at config.export.viewerUrl

script_generation.runtime: Node.js CommonJS — no ESM, no TypeScript, no build step
viewer_api_usage: GET /api/tree    → full doc tree (sections[]{ label, docs[], groups[] })
rendering_pipeline: If doc has no file → skip (virtual doc)
If docType is in SKIP_TYPES (source-code, ui-source-code, sql, schema-state, sample-data) → skip
If ext === 'json' AND catalogEntry.templateRef exists → normalize data + Mustache.render → Markdown
Otherwise → wrap raw content in fenced code block

normalizers_required.ui-module-spec: pages[] → add roles, requirements, menuVisibleLabel, hasActions, hasFilters, hasValidation booleans
normalizers_required.mde-command: flatten requires/produces objects to key-value lists
normalizers_required.mde-skill: flatten inputs/outputs objects to key-value lists
normalizers_required.all others: pass data through unchanged — template handles it

yml.nav_format:
- SectionLabel:
  - DocLabel: section/doc.md
  - GroupLabel:
    - DocLabel: section/group/doc.md

confluence_output.body_format: { type:'page', title, space:{key}, ancestors:[{id:parentId}], body:{storage:{value:html, representation:'storage'}} }
confluence_output.html_conversion: Convert rendered Markdown to HTML using marked npm package
confluence_output.hierarchy: One root page per export run, one section page per tree section, one content page per doc
confluence_output.requires_config: config.export.confluence: baseUrl, spaceKey, email, apiToken — abort with clear message if missing

error_handling: If viewer is unreachable → abort with: 'Cannot reach viewer at {url} — is it running?'
If a doc fails to render → log [skip] docLabel: reason, continue
If Confluence page creation fails → log [fail] title: error, continue
Print per-doc [ok]/[skip]/[fail] lines for CI visibility

rules: CRITICAL: Script must be pure CommonJS — no import/export, no TypeScript.
CRITICAL: Use the viewer's /api/tree and /api/catalog to discover all docs — never hardcode paths.
CRITICAL: Patch package.json by reading and merging — never overwrite.
CRITICAL: Patch configuration.json by reading and merging — never overwrite existing keys.
CRITICAL: Confluence auth credentials go in configuration.json with empty defaults — never hardcode tokens.
Template rendering must use the mustache npm package (require('mustache')).
Confluence HTML conversion must use the marked npm package (require('marked')).
Screenshots are copied from config.screenshots.outputDir to docs/assets/screenshots/.
mkdocs.yml nav must mirror the tree hierarchy from /api/tree.
Skipped doc types: source-code, ui-source-code, sql, schema-state, sample-data — these are too verbose for documentation.
